# split_h_2p_demo -DROTATE — Audit Protocol audit-1

**Branch/commit audited:** `claude/persp-c-horiz-review-ntqlca` @ `58d7343` (the `-DROTATE` extension of the already-audited-CLEAN `split_h_2p_demo` rail: `main.asm` +193/−37, variant script, `gen_assets.py`, 3 new committed assets, +2 tests, guide/roadmap/provenance).
**Role:** independent AUDIT-1 — I did not build this increment. All evidence below regenerated by me from a fresh materialization (`dryrun_split.sh /tmp/kit_audit_rot`), all measurements on the cycle-accurate emulator via MesenRunner; `/home/user/SuperForge` untouched (`git status` clean, HEAD = 58d7343; adversarial builds assembled in a scratch dir outside both trees, after the gates ran on the pristine kit).
**Method:** fresh materialize → build default + 6 variants → 18-test suite + 56-test split-family regression + all four gates → end-to-end read of the new ASM (width, API-block interplay, wrap arithmetic, VBlank bounding) → independent emulator probes (hardware DMA-register readback, 24- and 260-frame cadence lockstep incl. both heading wraps, per-frame pointer/movement verification against my own LUT parse, pivot feature-tracking over a 253° sweep) → adversarial gate-discrimination via a burdened build and a frozen-pointer build → independent asset regeneration + reimplementation of the `move64` math → ROM-image bank-layout byte-compare → visual inspection of my own renders.

## VERDICT: CLEAN (ship)

Every claim reproduced from scratch on the emulator. The headline holds and then some: the rotating loop stays in +1/+1 loop/NMI lockstep not just for the claimed 24 stepped frames but for **260 consecutive stepped frames**, spanning both heading wraps (H1 63→0 inc-wrap at step 200, H2 0→63 dec-wrap at step 142), with all four pose pointers equal to `$8000 + h·448` for the matching heading and both positions advancing by exactly `move64[h]` mod 1024 on **every single frame**. The per-channel indirect data banks read back from the live hardware registers as claimed ($43x7 = $02/$03). The bottom-centre pivot claim is confirmed both analytically and on the framebuffer (a world cell-corner feature stays pinned at bottom-centre across a 253° sweep, 16/16 grabs per band, while far features sweep and a control column stays cold). Both new tests demonstrably discriminate: a ~1.4-frame burdened rotate build fails the exact cadence gate at step 2 with the 30 Hz signature, and a frozen-pointer build reads sig-diff 0 (vs the >20 the render test requires). All three findings are info-severity; none require remediation before ship.

## Findings

1. **[info] Neither new test pins the pointer↔heading *binding*, nor the two rates/senses.** `tests/test_split_h_2p_demo.py:301-347` — the cadence test asserts H1/H2/positions *change*, and the render test asserts both bands' pixels *change*; a mix-up bug that drove both bands' pointers from H1 (same rate, same sense) would pass both. My probe pinned `IDX_AB+1/CD+1 = f(H1)` and `IDX_AB+4/CD+4 = f(H2)` on all 260 frames, so the shipped code is correct — the gap is suite discrimination, not the rail. Cheap hardening: assert the pointer formula against the WRAM headings inside the cadence test (4 `read_u16`s).
2. **[info] The ROTATE/RETARGET mutual exclusion is comment- and script-enforced only.** `main.asm:56-57` documents "Do not combine"; the variant script (verified) never combines them; but nothing stops a hand build — an `.ifdef ROTATE × RETARGET → .error` would make the constraint self-enforcing. If combined, both blocks write `IDX_*+4` in the same VBlank (ROTATE last-writer-wins except the frame-90 RETARGET stomp) — a silent misbuild, low impact.
3. **[info] `lorom_stream.cfg`'s banner comment still describes only the streaming rail's bank usage** (BANK2/BANK3 = "flat tilemap — STREAMS in"); this rail now links the same cfg with BANK2/BANK3 = pose blobs. Comment nit in a file this increment did not edit; worth a line when next touched. (Related pre-existing, unchanged observation: the header's mapping byte is $30 in *both* the default and rotate ROMs — shared `header.inc`, not this increment.)

**Verified with no findings (adversarial-pass highlights):**
- **Width discipline:** the entire ROTATE loop block is single-width A16/I16 with no transitions inside; `@no_h1`/`@no_h2` single-width-reachable; the RESET DASB stanza and both `set_indirect_banks` arms (`@set_ab`/`@set_cd`) push/pull in A16 around annotated `sep/rep` pairs, `@next` reached only in A16; `make width-check` clean (189 files).
- **`set_indirect_banks` generalization:** API_BLOCK_BASE+14/+15 sit inside the 16-slot × 4-byte marshal block ($60–$9F); `hdma_alloc.asm` touches only +0..+3 (and split_mask +8/+10/+12) — no collision; walk still covers CH0..CH7 exactly, terminates at $0100; DASB written per channel with DB=$00 (unchanged from the audited base).
- **Wrap arithmetic:** s16 `move64` delta + `and #$03FF` is exact mod-1024 (1024 divides 2^16) — verified on-emulator for negative deltas all 260 frames; H2 `dec/and #$3F` wrap observed live (0→63, pointer $EE40 = last pose, top boundary in-bounds: 63·448+448 = 28,672 = blob size exactly); `h·448` max $6E40 + $8000 = $EE40, no 16-bit carry.
- **VBlank bounding (claim 3 mechanism boundary):** instruction-accurate count of the ROTATE block worst case (H1-advance and H2-wrap coinciding): headings/divider ≈ 73 cycles + LUT movement 2×58 + shift-subtract pointer recompute 2×66 ≈ **321 CPU cycles**; whole loop incl. `stamp_origins` (~146) + counter + NMI ≈ **~550 CPU cycles ≈ 3 scanlines of the ~38-scanline VBlank window** (~8%) — two orders of magnitude inside a frame, corroborated by the 260-frame measured lockstep.
- **Bank/streaming layout:** rotate ROMs are 512 KB via `lorom_stream.cfg`; `poses64_ab.bin`/`poses64_cd.bin` (28,672 B = 64×448 each) byte-identical at LoROM file offsets $10000/$18000 (banks $02/$03), zero-fill after; default builds unchanged at 64 KB.
- **Boot state:** band 2 starts at heading 32 (word write, A16) with pointer `$8000+32·448 = $B800`; H1/CNT6 rely on the coldstart-cleared-WRAM contract verified in the base audit.

Hardware-register + WRAM probes (my scripts, rotate build, running ROM):

```
G_MSK (matrix)=0x000c  G_MSK2 (origin)=0x0030   -> matrix channels CH2 (AB) / CH3 (CD)
CH2 (AB): DMAP=$43 BBAD=$1B A1T=$C000 A1B=$7E DASB($43x7)=$02
CH3 (CD): DMAP=$43 BBAD=$1D A1T=$C010 A1B=$7E DASB($43x7)=$03
[PASS] cadence: 24 stepped frames, loop($7E:E030)/NMI($7E:E010) +1/+1 lockstep (final +24/+24)
[PASS] all four pose pointers == $8000 + h*448 for the matching heading, every sampled frame
[PASS] H1 (+$C06A) +1 (mod 64) exactly on frames with G_FRAMES%4==0
[PASS] H2 (+$C06C) -1 (mod 64) exactly on CNT6 wrap (every 6 frames, opposite sense)
[PASS] every frame: pos += move64[new heading] under the $03FF wrap, both cameras (s16 verified)
Extended run: step 142: H2 dec-wrap 0->63 (ptr $EE40 == $EE40) · step 200: H1 wrap 63->0 (ptrs ok)
[PASS] 260 stepped frames: loop/NMI +1/+1 lockstep throughout; pointers+movement exact on all 260
```

Pivot (rotfreeze, framebuffer; screenshots only at ≥12-frame hops per the harness hazard):

```
pivot-adjacent pixels across 3 hops x (33.75°/22.5°): band1 (128,sl111) = (0,123,0) all grabs;
  band2 (128,sl223) = (255,123,0) all grabs — byte-identical while both bands visibly sweep
feature track (both camera positions are world cell corners: 512=16*32, 768=24*32):
  cell edge nearest x=128 on each band's bottom scanline, 16 grabs sweeping h1 over 45 poses (253°):
  b1: pinned at x=128±3 in 14/16; pin OR centre-column transition (boundary-parallel case) 16/16
  b2: pinned in 10/16; pin OR centre-column 16/16;  control column x=64±3: only 5/16 and 2/16
  far edges wander 10-26+ px/hop (e.g. 25→20→35→47 and 230→234→219→208)
  (a band-centre pivot would displace the bottom-centre feature ~16 px per 3-pose hop — excluded)
analytic cross-check: at (SX,SY)=(128, band_bottom): SX+HOFS−M7X = 0 and SY+VOFS−M7Y = 0,
  so bottom-centre samples world (M7X,M7Y) = camera pos for ANY matrix — pivot by construction
method note: my first attempt (window MAD ratios 1.1-4.2x) was weakly discriminating — the
  checker cells at bottom scale (~50 screen px) swallow whole windows; replaced by feature tracking
```

Gate discrimination (my adversarial builds, assembled in scratch from a copied source, gates already run on the pristine tree):

```
rotate_burn14000 (dey/bne burn after stamp_origins, ~1.4-frame loop):
  exact cadence-gate procedure FAILS at step 2 (loop +1, NMI +2); 24 steps: loop +12 / NMI +24 (30 Hz)
rotfreeze_frozenptr (pointer-recompute block deleted; headings still advance):
  exact test_rot_both_bands_rotate metric: band1/band2 sig-diff = 0/0/0 across 3 hops (needs >20) — CAUGHT
  same build: h1/h2 still advance in WRAM -> the cadence test's guards alone would NOT catch a
  pointer freeze; the render test carries that load (division of labor confirmed; see finding 1)
```

Tooling + ROM image:

```
independent regen (fresh staged copy): all 8 assets byte-identical incl. poses64_ab/cd (28,672 B),
  move64.bin (256 B); move64 == my independent round(2·(−sin,−cos)) math (cardinals (0,−2),(−2,0),(0,2),(2,0))
poses64_ab[8·448:9·448] == committed pose_rot45_ab (the 45° retarget slice ties the sets together)
rotate .sfc: bank $02 file region == poses64_ab, bank $03 == poses64_cd (byte-compare), zero-fill tails
variant script: 6 variants, ROTATE never combined with RETARGET (read + build log)
```

Renders (mine, from my binaries, personally inspected, in `/tmp/audit_rot_shots/`): `rotfreeze_1/2.png` — two perspective checker floors at visibly different rotated headings, clean single-line seam; `rotate_render.png`/`rotate_render2.png` — full rotate+move build, both bands at distinct rotated headings that advance between grabs, seam sig-diff 45 (> the 40 threshold) at the modeled image row 119, no tearing.
